prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in the third quarter of this most bewildering year, the noble Company of Software-as-a-Service didst report a revenue of $100 million, a sum most fantastical and beyond the comprehension of mortal men. 'Tis said that their subscribers, a legion of faceless account-holders, didst increase by 30%, a prodigy of arithmetic that doth strain credulity. Furthermore, the company's expenditures on "research and development" didst balloon to $50 million, a Однак of gold coin sufficient to construct a castle of unparalleled magnificence, yet wherefore, I dare not hazard a guess. In conclusion, the company's "EBITDA margin" didst narrow by 5%, a portent of doom or triumph, I know not which, for in this mystical realm of commerce, the unwary chronicler doth stumble as in a dark and foreboding forest.
